description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Fukushima Prefecture (福島県, Fukushima-ken; pronounced [ɸɯ̥.kɯꜜ.ɕi.ma, -kɯ.ɕi.maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Tōhoku region of Honshu.

description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: city in Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Iwakuni (岩国市, Iwakuni-shi) is a city located in Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: city in and county seat of Honolulu County, Hawaii, United States, that is also the capital city of Hawaii
description_wiki: Honolulu (  HON-ə-LOO-loo; Hawaiian: [honoˈlulu]) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Hawaii, located in the Pacific Ocean.

description: county seat of Madera County, California, United States
description_wiki: Madera (Spanish for "lumber") is a city in, and the county seat of, Madera County, located in the San Joaquin Valley of California.

description: city in Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Selma is a city in Fresno County, California.

description: city in Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Parlier is a city in Fresno County, California, United States.

description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: Yakuza group
school that teaches the Japanese language to non-native speakers
California statute
description_wiki: The California Alien Land Law of 1913 (also known as the Webb–Haney Act) prohibited "aliens ineligible for citizenship" from owning agricultural land or possessing long-term leases over it, but permitted leases lasting up to three years.

description: dissolved municipality in Kitaazumi district, Nagano prefecture, Japan
Japanese family name (小此木)
1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: Japanese American internment camp
description_wiki: The Jerome War Relocation Center was a Japanese American internment camp located in southeastern Arkansas, near the town of Jerome in the Arkansas Delta.

description: major mountain range in western North America
description_wiki: The Rocky Mountains, also known as the Rockies, are a major mountain range and the largest mountain system in North America.

description: infantry regiment of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 442nd Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Army.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.
